list.name: run
list.label: Run (test)
list.query: SELECT id AS __value, dataset || ' #' || id AS __text FROM runs ORDER BY id DESC

options.content: Adapted from **arena-hard-auto**: do judge scores reward *quality*, or just *length / markdown formatting*? These panels are an **association** diagnostic, **not** a causal de-bias — length is confounded with difficulty (hard prompts legitimately run longer), and absolute judging can't difference difficulty out the way arena-hard's pairwise design does. For the OLS **style-adjusted ranking** (judge scores controlled for length + markdown, with model fixed effects), run `python -m app.style_analysis`.

panels.title: Judge-score ↔ style correlation per model
panels.description: Per-model Pearson correlation of judge_score with response length and markdown counts (judged rows only). Positive ⇒ this model's scores MAY be inflated by verbosity/formatting. Association, not causation. Source: style_confound (view 012).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT model, n_judged, corr_score_len, corr_score_headers, corr_score_bold, corr_score_lists
FROM style_confound
WHERE run_id = $run
ORDER BY corr_score_len DESC NULLS LAST;

panels.title: Raw judge score vs response style averages per model
panels.description: Quality next to how long / formatted each model's answers are (judged rows only). Lets you eyeball whether high scorers are also the most verbose/formatted.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT m.name AS model,
       AVG(res.judge_score)::NUMERIC(3,1)               AS avg_judge,
       AVG(res.output_tokens)::INT                      AS avg_tokens,
       AVG(res.resp_style_headers)::NUMERIC(5,1)        AS avg_headers,
       AVG(res.resp_style_bold)::NUMERIC(5,1)           AS avg_bold,
       AVG(res.resp_style_ordered + res.resp_style_unordered)::NUMERIC(5,1) AS avg_list_items
FROM results res JOIN models m ON m.id = res.model_id
WHERE res.run_id = $run AND res.judge_score IS NOT NULL AND res.resp_style_headers IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY m.name
ORDER BY avg_judge DESC;
